_comment: Example model catalog for codex-bridge. Copy to ~/.codex/proxy-models.json (or any path) and point both Codex (config.toml -> model_catalog_json) and codex-bridge (.env -> MODEL_CATALOG_PATH) at it. The proxy infers the upstream provider from each slug prefix (deepseek-* / mimo-* / gpt-*).

_field_reference.slug: Model identifier sent by Codex CLI. The proxy uses the prefix (deepseek-* / mimo-* / gpt-*) to route to the correct upstream provider.
_field_reference.display_name: Human-readable name shown in the Codex CLI model picker.
_field_reference.supported_reasoning_levels: Array of { effort, description } objects. 'effort' is one of: none | minimal | low | medium | high | xhigh. Codex maps the user's reasoning-effort slider to these values; the proxy then translates them to each provider's native format (see README → Reasoning Effort Translation).
_field_reference.shell_type: Shell environment for Codex CLI sandbox. 'default' uses the system shell.
_field_reference.visibility: 'list' = shown in model picker; 'hidden' = available but not listed.
_field_reference.supported_in_api: true = the model can be called via the API. Set false to list-only (informational).
_field_reference.priority: Sort order in the model picker. Lower number = higher priority (shown first).
_field_reference.base_instructions: Optional system-prompt prefix injected by Codex for this model. Leave empty for default behavior.
_field_reference.supports_reasoning_summaries: Whether the model returns summarized reasoning steps (currently only some OpenAI models).
_field_reference.support_verbosity: Whether Codex should send verbosity hints to this model.
_field_reference.truncation_policy: Context-window truncation strategy. 'mode' is 'tokens'; 'limit' is the max token count before Codex truncates older messages.
_field_reference.supports_parallel_tool_calls: true = Codex may issue multiple tool calls in a single turn for this model.
_field_reference.experimental_supported_tools: Array of experimental tool identifiers this model opts into. Usually empty.
